core_mechanism: Weyl-quantization of a noncommutative operator algebra defined by fixed commutation relations (here a solvable/affine Lie algebra in which one generator dilates the others), via classifying the algebra's regular representations and building an operator-symbol calculus with a trace functional and the induced uncertainty relations between noncommuting observables
brief_reason: The abstract classifies the regular representations of the relations [T,X_j]=(i/k)X_j, canonically Weyl-quantizes them into a symbolic calculus carrying a trace functional, and derives coordinate uncertainty relations, which is precisely the representation-theoretic quantization of an algebra fixed by its commutators.
